task.title: Fix Intermittent Data Aggregation Test
task.description: test_batch_aggregation occasionally produces inconsistent totals across runs.
constraints: Do not aggregate more than 50 batches

steps.observation: Test expects deterministic sums but occasionally differs by 1-2 units.
Batch aggregation uses a Python list append + sort before summing.

steps.thought: Reproduce flakiness by running test a few times.
steps.observation: Intermittent mismatch reproduced on second run.

steps.thought: Run a controlled aggregation of 20 batches (within the 50-batch limit).
steps.observation: Observed that ordering of items varies between runs.

steps.thought: Sorting is done using Python's default sorted(), but batches contain mutable dicts.
steps.observation: Indeed, sort key is not fixed — inconsistent ordering.

steps.thought: Fix: provide a deterministic key in sorting.
Re-run the flaky test to confirm stability.

steps.observation: Aggregated totals are consistent on each run.

arguments.message: Identified and resolved batch aggregation issue.
